title: thymus serpyllum f. longifolius
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. longifolius, also known as Thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus serpyllum plant, which is a low-growing, flowering perennial herb native to Europe and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Thymus serpyllum f. longifolius include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum f. longifolius include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Thymus serpyllum f. longifolius can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or dried herb, which can be used to make cough syrups, salves, and other topical applications.
